action.reasoning: Observation: 15M model (0.0763) is worse than 5M model (0.1307). The 5M model had FourRooms 56%, DoorKey-16x16 28%. The 15M model regressed significantly. Analysis: The larger network (512,512,256) with ent_coef=0.05 likely caused training instability or the model overfit. The best checkpoint is ppo_minigrid_5000000_steps.zip. KeyCorridorS3R3, LockedRoom, MultiRoom-N6, Fetch all remain at 0% - these need specific skills. Decision: Resume from the 5M checkpoint with better hyperparameters - smaller network, lower learning rate, moderate entropy. Also try to improve by training more carefully. With 4.6h left and ~13 iterations, I should train for ~10M more steps from the 5M checkpoint. Let me also check what the 5M checkpoint architecture was - it used net_arch=[dict(pi=[256,256], vf=[256,256])] from iter 10. I'll resume from that and train carefully.
